channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Three-dimensional photonic topological insulators go unbounded
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02559-w

Three-dimensional all-dielectric photonic topological insulators are experimentally shown to control far-field emission via the pseudo-spin degree of freedom.
item.dc:title: Three-dimensional photonic topological insulators go unbounded
item.dc:creator: Eran Lustig

item.title: Optically detected nuclear magnetic resonance of coherent spins in a molecular complex
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02539-0

Optical initialization and read-out of nuclear spins with a spin coherence time of 2 ms in a Eu3+ molecular crystal is demonstrated with radio-frequency control, providing coherent nuclear spin manipulation for quantum information technologies.
item.dc:title: Optically detected nuclear magnetic resonance of coherent spins in a molecular complex
item.dc:creator: Evgenij Vasilenko
Vishnu Unni Chorakkunnath
Jeremias Resch
Nicholas Jobbitt
Diana Serrano
Philippe Goldner
Senthil Kumar Kuppusamy
Mario Ruben
David Hunger

item.title: Multiscale phase nucleation driven by photoinduced polarons in a volume-changing material
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02521-w

Photoinduced phase transitions in a Prussian blue analogue are studied using femtosecond X-ray techniques, revealing a multistep phase nucleation process with photoinduced polarons triggering the macroscopic phase transformation.
item.dc:title: Multiscale phase nucleation driven by photoinduced polarons in a volume-changing material
item.dc:creator: Marius Hervé
Gaël Privault
Serhane Zerdane
Shintaro Akagi
Leland B. Gee
Ryan D. Ribson
Matthieu Chollet
Shin-ichi Ohkoshi
Hiroko Tokoro
Marco Cammarata
Eric Collet

item.title: Author Correction: An iontronic reservoir for highly robust neuromorphic prosthesis
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02579-6

Author Correction: An iontronic reservoir for highly robust neuromorphic prosthesis
item.dc:title: Author Correction: An iontronic reservoir for highly robust neuromorphic prosthesis
item.dc:creator: Mengjiao Pei
Tian Gao
Li Liu
Wenlong Li
Haotian Long
Yifei Luo

Hangyuan Cui

item.title: Non-monotonic magnetic friction from collective rotor dynamics
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 18 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02538-1

The forces and dissipative losses of an array of rotatable magnets moving above a commensurate array of fixed macroscopic magnets are measured. Modelling and analysis show a maximum of dissipation at an intermediate distance where ferro- and antiferromagnetic order compete.
item.dc:title: Non-monotonic magnetic friction from collective rotor dynamics
item.dc:creator: Hongri Gu
Anton Lüders
Clemens Bechinger

item.title: Linking metal oxidation to oxygen reactivity
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 17 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02540-7

Using multiple spectroscopies, it is shown that water oxidation on iridium oxides is driven by accumulated oxygen ligand holes.
item.dc:title: Linking metal oxidation to oxygen reactivity
item.dc:creator: A. R. Muñoz
T. E. Jones
